edges.description: Dominant-negative P5CS mutations reduce enzyme activity and delay cellular proline accumulation.
edges.target: Reduced P5CS enzymatic activity and delayed proline accumulation

edges.description: Delayed proline accumulation is the cellular biochemical readout of reduced P5CS activity.
edges.source: Reduced P5CS enzymatic activity and delayed proline accumulation

edges.description: Impaired P5CS-dependent proline metabolism perturbs collagen-linked connective tissue integrity.
edges.source: Reduced P5CS enzymatic activity and delayed proline accumulation

edges.description: The same ALDH18A1-ADCL metabolic defect is associated with neurodevelopmental involvement.
edges.source: Reduced P5CS enzymatic activity and delayed proline accumulation

edges.description: The ALDH18A1-ADCL metabolic defect is associated with cataract or corneal opacity.
edges.source: Reduced P5CS enzymatic activity and delayed proline accumulation

edges.description: Collagen-linked connective tissue vulnerability contributes to the lax, wrinkled skin phenotype.
Connective tissue matrix vulnerability is associated with joint laxity and hypermobility.
Connective tissue laxity is associated with hip dislocation in reported ALDH18A1-ADCL cases.
Connective tissue involvement is associated with joint contractures in dominant ALDH18A1 cutis laxa.
Connective tissue matrix vulnerability is associated with cranial vessel tortuosity in reported ALDH18A1-ADCL cases.
Developmental growth involvement can include prenatal intrauterine growth retardation.
Developmental involvement includes prenatal or postnatal growth delay.
Neurodevelopmental involvement is associated with hypotonia.
Neurodevelopmental involvement includes reduced head circumference.
Neurodevelopmental involvement includes psychomotor retardation or global developmental delay.
Neuroimaging in dominant ALDH18A1 cutis laxa can show cerebral white matter signal abnormality.
Ocular involvement is expressed as cataract or corneal clouding.

evidence.reference_title: Recurrent De Novo Mutations Affecting Residue Arg138 of Pyrroline-5-Carboxylate Synthase Cause a Progeroid Form of Autosomal-Dominant Cutis Laxa.
evidence.snippet: We found three heterozygous mutations in ALDH18A1 leading to amino acid substitutions of the same highly conserved residue, Arg138 in P5CS.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal dominant cutis laxa with progeroid features due to a novel, de novo mutation in ALDH18A1.
evidence.snippet: This is the first report of an individual with ALDH18A1-ADCL due to a substitution at a residue other than p.Arg138.

nodes.description: ADCL3 includes neurodevelopmental involvement with growth delay, microcephaly, hypotonia, and psychomotor or global developmental delay, although the intermediate mechanism from the proline/collagen metabolic defect remains incompletely defined.

evidence.reference_title: A novel case of autosomal-dominant cutis laxa caused by a de novo likely pathogenic variant in ALDH18A1: case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: growth delay, hypotonia, joint laxity, lax skin, cataract, dysmorphic features, microcephaly, cranial vessel tortuosity, hip dislocation, and psychomotor retardation.

nodes.description: Cataract or corneal opacity occurs in ALDH18A1-associated dominant cutis laxa; the specific tissue mechanism downstream of the proline/collagen metabolic defect is not resolved from cached abstract evidence.

evidence.reference_title: A novel case of autosomal-dominant cutis laxa caused by a de novo likely pathogenic variant in ALDH18A1: case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: lax skin, cataract, dysmorphic features

nodes.description: Cataracts or corneal clouding present in the majority of affected individuals, often requiring surgical intervention.

evidence.reference_title: A novel case of autosomal-dominant cutis laxa caused by a de novo likely pathogenic variant in ALDH18A1: case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: lax skin, cataract, dysmorphic features

nodes.description: Increased cerebral white matter T2 signal has been reported on neuroimaging in dominant ALDH18A1-associated cutis laxa.

evidence.reference_title: Imaging in cutis laxa syndrome caused by a dominant negative ALDH18A1 mutation, with hypotheses for intracranial vascular tortuosity and wide perivascular spaces.
evidence.snippet: increased T2 signal intensity in the cerebral white matter over time

nodes.description: Tortuosity of cranial vessels has been reported in ALDH18A1-associated autosomal dominant cutis laxa.

evidence.reference_title: A novel case of autosomal-dominant cutis laxa caused by a de novo likely pathogenic variant in ALDH18A1: case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: microcephaly, cranial vessel tortuosity, hip dislocation

nodes.description: Thin, lax, wrinkled skin with visible veins giving a prematurely aged appearance. All reported patients exhibit this finding.

evidence.reference_title: Recurrent De Novo Mutations Affecting Residue Arg138 of Pyrroline-5-Carboxylate Synthase Cause a Progeroid Form of Autosomal-Dominant Cutis Laxa.
evidence.snippet: recurrent de novo mutations, affecting the highly conserved residue Arg138 of P5CS, cause an autosomal-dominant form of cutis laxa with progeroid features

nodes.description: Heterozygous Arg138 mutations produce a stable P5CS protein that incorporates into P5CS oligomers but disrupts their structure or composition, resulting in a dominant-negative effect. This reduces P5CS enzymatic activity and impairs the mitochondrial proline cycle.

evidence.reference_title: Recurrent De Novo Mutations Affecting Residue Arg138 of Pyrroline-5-Carboxylate Synthase Cause a Progeroid Form of Autosomal-Dominant Cutis Laxa.
evidence.snippet: we found that the mutant cells had a reduced P5CS enzymatic activity leading to a delayed proline accumulation

evidence.reference_title: Recurrent De Novo Mutations Affecting Residue Arg138 of Pyrroline-5-Carboxylate Synthase Cause a Progeroid Form of Autosomal-Dominant Cutis Laxa.
evidence.snippet: the P5CS-p.Arg138Trp protein was stable and able to interact with wild-type P5CS but showed an altered sub-mitochondrial distribution

evidence.reference_title: Structural basis of dynamic P5CS filaments.
evidence.snippet: The bifunctional enzyme Δ1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate synthase (P5CS) is vital to the synthesis of proline and ornithine

nodes.description: ALDH18A1/P5CS is tied to proline and collagen metabolism, providing a plausible connective-tissue branch for lax skin and joint laxity in ADCL3.

evidence.reference_title: A novel case of autosomal-dominant cutis laxa caused by a de novo likely pathogenic variant in ALDH18A1: case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: including ALDH18A1, which encodes Δ1-pyrroline-5-carboxylate synthetase, a key enzyme in proline and collagen metabolism.

nodes.description: P5CS Arg138 mutant fibroblasts show reduced enzymatic activity with delayed proline accumulation, placing ADCL3 in the ALDH18A1-linked proline biosynthesis disease spectrum.
